title: Microterangis oligantha
taxonomy: Microterangis oligantha belongs to the family Epidendroideae, which is a subfamily of orchids. It is also classified as a member of the genus Microterangis, which contains several species of terrestrial orchids. Microterangis oligantha is native to Madagascar, a country with a rich diversity of flora and fauna. This species is classified as an epiphyte, meaning it grows on other plants without deriving nutrients from them. Microterangis oligantha is a relatively rare and lesser-known species within the orchid family.
uses: Microterangis oligantha is reported to have potential medicinal properties, but further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and applications.
